allow: PowerShell($vs = @\(\); $vsPaths = @\('C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\2022', 'C:\\Program Files \(x86\)\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\2022'\); foreach \($p in $vsPaths\) { if \(Test-Path $p\) { Get-ChildItem $p -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $vs += "$\($_.Name\) at $\($_.FullName\)" } } }; if \($vs.Count -eq 0\) { "VS2022: not found in standard locations" } else { "VS2022: $\($vs -join '; '\)" }; "---"; $vswhere = 'C:\\Program Files \(x86\)\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\Installer\\vswhere.exe'; if \(Test-Path $vswhere\) { & $vswhere -latest -format json | ConvertFrom-Json | Select-Object displayName, installationVersion, installationPath | Format-List })
